title: relative_time is explained in the example, but it is deprecated
body:
### Feedback

The “relative_time” function is explained in the example, but according to the detailed documentation, it is deprecated. The “time_since” function, which is recommended as its successor, is only mentioned.

I think it would be better to use “time_since” in the example.

### URL

https://www.home-assistant.io/docs/templating/dates-and-times/

### Version

2026.5.0

### Additional information

_No response_
label: docs

title: Delayed automations stop after restart
body:
### The problem

I have automations that are supposed to perform an action after a delay. For example, the irrigation pump for my garden is supposed to switch off again after a certain time after it starts.

If Home Assistant is restarted during the delay period, this automation isn't terminated. In my case, the garden irrigation ran for two hours until I noticed that half the patio was flooded.

A test with a quickly created automation, where I restarted Home Assistant while the delay was running, also showed this behavior.

Since Home Assistant has to be restarted almost daily due to updates, this behavior can occur frequently. I can't check whether any automations are still running after every update. Such automations need to be restored to their pre-update state after an update.

### What version of Home Assistant Core has the issue?

2026.3.3

### What was the last working version of Home Assistant Core?

_No response_

### What type of installation are you running?

Home Assistant OS

### Integration causing the issue

_No response_

### Link to integration documentation on our website

_No response_

### Diagnostics information

_No response_

### Example YAML snippet

```yaml
alias: Test
description: ""
triggers:
  - device_id: 946eba3f42306c1bd9df668367c74959
    domain: zha
    type: remote_button_long_press
    subtype: button_4
    trigger: device
conditions: []
actions:
  - delay:
      hours: 0
      minutes: 5
      seconds: 0
      milliseconds: 0
  - action:
label: feature

title: Feature Request: Add Amazon Music URI playback support for Sonos integration (parity with Spotify)
body:
## Feature Request

### Problem

The Sonos integration currently supports direct URI playback for Spotify — both `spotify:playlist:<id>` and `https://open.spotify.com/...` URLs work natively with `media_player.play_media`. Amazon Music has no equivalent support. Attempting to play Amazon Music content via `https://music.amazon.com/playlists/<id>` or a hypothetical `amazon-music:playlist:<id>` URI scheme returns HTTP 500.

This means there is no way to programmatically play Amazon Music content from automations without first manually saving every item as a Sonos favorite.

### Current behavior

Calling `media_player.play_media` with an Amazon Music URL or URI-style identifier fails with HTTP 500. No playback is initiated.

### Expected behavior

Amazon Music content should be playable via `media_player.play_media` using standard identifiers (ASIN, share URL, or a URI scheme like `amazon-music:playlist:<id>`), similar to how Spotify URIs are handled today.

### Workaround

Save Amazon Music content as Sonos Favorites in the Sonos app, then play via:
```yaml
service: media_player.play_media
data:
  media_content_type: "favorite_item_id"
  media_content_id: "FV:2/<id>"
```

This works but requires manual pre-curation of every piece of content and prevents dynamic/programmatic playback.

### Technical context

When Amazon Music content plays on Sonos (via favorites), the internal content ID structure is:
```
x-sonosapi-hls-static:catalog/tracks/<ASIN>/<session-uuids>/<account-id>/
label: feature

title: Add max Battery input
body:
### Feedback

In cases you have a battery that has the solar cells as DC input the forecast is wrong because you can only deliver 800w into the grid but still load the battery with the overshoot power.
For these cases it should be possible to not only enter the power of the inverter but the max loading power of the battery and its capacity.

### URL

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/forecast_solar/

### Version

2026.4.4

title: Comelit: integration loses devices after HA 2026.1 update
body:
[Log_2026.1.3.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25076532/Log_2026.1.3.log)

### The problem

Since updating Home Assistant to version 2026.1 after approximately 20 minutes of uptime, all entities associated with the Comelit Web Server (loads, climate probes, etc.) disappear or become unavailable.
The VEDO alarm section remains perfectly functional and does not suffer from this disconnection.
To restore the entities, it is currently necessary to:

Restart Home Assistant.
OR reconfigure/reload the Comelit integration from the "Devices & Services" menu. However, the issue reoccurs after another 20 minutes.

HA Version: 2026.1 (and 2026.1.x)
Integration: Comelit (Official)
Fresh/Clean installation of HA (issue persists, ruling out custom component conflicts).

title: Integration Govee lights local: Inconsistency in H600D Model Support
body:
The official [Govee Lights Local](https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/govee_light_local/) integration for Home Assistant (HA) lists the H600D model among its supported devices.

However, the H600D model is not included in the official list of Govee devices that support LAN API Control (Local Area Network), as can be verified on the official Govee support page:

🔗 Official Govee Reference Link: https://app-h5.govee.com/user-manual/wlan-guide

This discrepancy suggests that the information provided in the Home Assistant integration documentation is either inaccurate or potentially misleading.

Question/Clarification:

Am I misunderstanding the official Govee documentation? If the H600D model is not officially supported by the LAN API, is there perhaps an unofficial way (or a workaround) to enable LAN control for this specific device?
label: question

title: Calendar platform: missing delete_event/update_event actions and uid not returned by get_events service
body:
## Problem

The calendar platform currently only exposes `calendar.create_event` and `calendar.get_events` as actions. There is no way to programmatically **delete** or **update** calendar events.

The frontend can delete events via the WebSocket command `calendar/event/delete`, but this is not exposed as a service/action and is not accessible via the REST API.

Additionally, the `calendar.get_events` service (called via `POST /api/services/calendar/get_events?return_response`) does **not** return the `uid` field for events. However, the REST endpoint `GET /api/calendars/<entity_id>?start=...&end=...` **does** return `uid`. This inconsistency makes it impossible to identify events for deletion/update even if those actions existed.

## Expected behavior

1. **`calendar.delete_event`** and **`calendar.update_event`** should be available as actions, accepting `entity_id` and `uid` (and updated fields for update).
2. **`calendar.get_events`** should return `uid` in its response, consistent with the REST GET endpoint.

## Use case

I import work shifts from a Google Sheets document into a `local_calendar` via an n8n workflow. When shifts change, I need to clear the existing events for a given month and re-import them. Without `delete_event` as an action, the only workaround is directly manipulating the `.ics` file and reloading the integration — which is fragile and undocumented.

## Environment

- Home Assistant Core 2025.12+
- Affects at least: `local_calendar`, likely all calen
label: feature

title: `kelvin` vs `color_temp_kelvin`
body:
### The problem

This is not as much a bug report as a rant or complain. Perhaps a call for more visibility in the docs.

I update core often. Usually every month. I updated to latest core today. Suddenly all my light switches stopped working. It took me a while to debug the problem, slowly finding out that only the automation to turn lights on was broken.

The cause was I set `kelvin` to a neutral temp when turning on lights. This setting was deprecated in favor of `color_temp_kelvin`, causing all switches to error out.

It would be beneficial for users to make these changes more visible in docs. I've found [this](https://developers.home-assistant.io/blog/2026/02/23/remove-deprecate-light-features/) after, but this is something I'd expect not to break my whole home light setup.

Feel free to close if you think there is no action or the docs notice was enough. ;)

### What version of Home Assistant Core has the issue?

2026.2.2

### What was the last working version of Home Assistant Core?

2026.3.3

### What type of installation are you running?

Home Assistant OS

### Example YAML snippet

```
alias: mando_luz_despacho
description: ""
use_blueprint:
  path: damru/ikea-rodret_E2201_ZHA-Z2M_control-anything.yaml
  input:
    remote_name: mando_luz_despacho
    on_press_action:
      - metadata: {}
        data:
          brightness_pct: 100
          color_temp_kelvin: 2700
        target:
          entity_id: light.luz_despacho
        action: light.turn_on
    off_press_act
label: question

title: Door Lock Operating Mode unsupported modes are displayed in the list.
body:
### The problem

The `DoorLock` `OperatingMode` selector includes all 5 operating modes unconditionally, but the Matter specification uses a bitmap attribute (`SupportedOperatingModes`) to indicate which modes a device actually supports.

The mistake was introduced by this PR:
- https://github.com/home-assistant/core/pull/157132

### What version of Home Assistant Core has the issue?

core-2025-12

### What was the last working version of Home Assistant Core?

_No response_

### What type of installation are you running?

Home Assistant OS

### Integration causing the issue

matter

### Link to integration documentation on our website

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/matter/

### Diagnostics information

_No response_

### Anything in the logs that might be useful for us?


### Additional information

**Data model**:
https://github.com/project-chip/connectedhomeip/blob/master/data_model/1.4.2/clusters/DoorLock.xml
label: bug
